title: Ensure that 'Allow access only via SSL' is set to 'Yes'

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Allow access only via SSL' is set to 'Yes'
input_row.description: Setting ‘Allow access only via SSL’ to ‘Yes’ ensures that data in transit to and from Azure Cache for Redis is encrypted using TLS.
input_row.rationale: Data in transit which is not encrypted is vulnerable to attacks including adversary-in-the- middle (AITM or MITM), eavesdropping, or session hijack. These attacks can result in the compromise and exfiltration of data.
input_row.audit: From Azure Portal 1. Search for and open the Azure Cache for Redis service 2. For each instance, repeat the remaining steps 3. Click on the name of the instance 4. In the blade menu on the left, under Settings, click on Advanced Settings 5. Review the setting under Allow access only via SSL If Yes is selected, the configuration for that instance is compliant. From Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 22bee202-a82f-4305-9a2a-6d7f44d4dedb - Name: 'Only secure connections to your Azure Cache for Redis should be enabled'
input_row.remediation: From Azure Portal 1. Search for and open the Azure Cache for Redis service 2. For each instance, repeat the remaining steps 3. Click on the name of the instance 4. In the blade menu on the left, under Settings, click on Advanced Settings 5. Select Yes under the Allow access only via SSL heading
input_row.impact: No additional cost is required to implement this recommendation. Aside from expected network changes (no unencrypted communications), performance should not be impacted.
input_row.default_value: By default, 'Allow access only via SSL' is set to 'Yes.'

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_elasticache_cache_security_allow_ssl_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- The automated program would check the configurations of AWS ElastiCache (specifically for Redis) to ensure that SSL connections are enforced.
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws elasticache describe-cache-clusters --show-cache-node-info` to retrieve information about the cache clusters. This will provide information on whether the configuration requires the usage of SSL.
- Use the boto3 SDK method `elasticache.describe_cache_clusters()` to access the same information programmatically.
- The program should enumerate all ElastiCache clusters in the specified accounts and regions.
- Validate the `AuthTokenEnabled` parameter to ensure it is set correctly, as well as checking `TransitEncryptionEnabled` to verify that traffic is encrypted.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: If `TransitEncryptionEnabled` is `True` for each cache cluster.
  - Fail: If `TransitEncryptionEnabled` is `False` for any cache cluster.
- Additionally, check the `EngineVersion` to ensure it supports SSL if other checks indicate SSL needs to be enabled. 

This comprehensive approach ensures that the updated configurations in AWS ElastiCache are in compliance with security best practices regarding SSL connections.
